title: Unicode Normalization, Case Folding, and Original Evidence · Guided Program Studio
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds versioned reversible Unicode policy from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned reversible Unicode policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose NFC, NFD, NFKC, NFKD, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision.
Use casefold for declared caseless matching and test expansions and language-sensitive limitations rather than assuming lower is equivalent.
Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Normalization Form
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 7772 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Normalization Form.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose nfc, nfd, nfkc, nfkd, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision. For Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Normalization Form, keep Normalization Form connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence-n5oro0
# Competency: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence.
# Case 3758: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_unicode_version_evidence_n5oro0(original_units: int = 9, normalized_units: int = 8, form: str = "NFC", original_kept: bool = True, unicode_version_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_n5oro0 = "n5oro0-0oro5n-3758"
    assert evidence_variant_n5oro0
    evidence_axes_n5oro0 = (
        "corpusn5oro0",
        "policy0oro5n",
        "revision3758",
        "commandn5oro03758",
        "report0oro5n3758",
        "test3758n5oro0",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_n5oro0)) == 6
    if original_units < 0 or normalized_units < 0:
        return False, normalized_units, "unicode-count"
    if form not in {"NFC", "NFD", "NFKC", "NFKD", "NONE"}:
        return False, normalized_units, "normalization-form"
    if not original_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "lost-evidence"
    if not unicode_version_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "unversioned-policy"
    assert original_units >= 0
    return True, normalized_units, "reversible-unicode"
